level_signals: Base pay range $104,000–$156,000 USD, consistent with mid-to-senior IC

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 4–8+ years of product management experience building customer-facing or platform products.
Comfortable navigating complex tradeoffs and aligning stakeholders across engineering, design, support, and go-to-market teams.
Strong cross-functional communicator who can translate customer problems into clear product requirements and decisions.
Data-driven and comfortable using metrics and experimentation to inform product decisions.
Passionate about building intuitive, high-quality customer experiences that scale.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience building or improving customer support, self-service, or portal-style products is highly desirable.

scope.surface: Customer Hub — shopper-facing self-service portal for post-purchase experiences (order tracking, account management, loyalty benefits, support handoff to agent workflows)
core_responsibilities: Contribute to the product vision and roadmap for the Customer Hub, a self-serve portal where shoppers interact with brands.
Design and improve self-service experiences that allow shoppers to manage common needs such as order tracking, account management, loyalty benefits, and support inquiries.
Define the handoff from self-serve experiences to agent workflows, ensuring smooth transitions when shoppers require human support.
Partner closely with engineering, design, data, and customer experience teams to deliver scalable and reliable solutions.
Balance the needs of shopper-facing UX, brand operators, and internal support agents to create cohesive end-to-end experiences.
Drive product discovery through customer research, data analysis, and experimentation to identify opportunities to improve resolution rates and customer satisfaction.
Define and monitor success metrics across self-serve adoption, resolution rates, customer satisfaction, and support efficiency.
Communicate priorities, tradeoffs, and product strategy clearly across cross-functional stakeholders.

compensation.equity_form: equity mentioned as part of total compensation package

green_flags: Equity and bonus explicitly mentioned as part of total comp
Clear product surface and ownership area (Customer Hub, post-purchase self-service)
Explicitly encourages close-but-not-exact matches to apply
Only up to 10% travel required — largely flexible location
red_flags: Location type is ambiguous — JD references US geographic markets and salary differentials by location but does not specify whether the role is remote, hybrid, or onsite
Visa sponsorship status not addressed anywhere in the JD
4–8+ year range is unusually wide and may indicate uncertainty about level

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range and years range)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and company context)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed)
location.type (inferred as hybrid based on travel mention and multi-market salary language; not explicitly stated)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred from 'cost of labor across various U.S. geographic markets' language)
ambiguities: Location type is not explicitly stated as remote, hybrid, or onsite. The JD references US geographic salary markets and up to 10% travel, suggesting a distributed/remote-eligible role, but no definitive statement is made. Treated as hybrid here.
Visa sponsorship is not mentioned anywhere in the JD; left as 'unclear'.
The '4–8+' year range is unusual — both 4 and 8+ are listed together, making seniority level ambiguous. Could indicate openness to mid-level through senior candidates.
